files_modified: src/cli/coderef-mcp-server.ts (P1, P2, P3, P4 — new tools + loaders)
src/cli/coderef-watch.ts (P5 — pass changed-file set to incremental populate)
src/pipeline/orchestrator.ts (P4 in-process build path; P5 delta-resolve wiring)
files_created: src/pipeline/symbol-table-cache.ts (P5 — persisted full project-wide symbol table; name provisional)
__tests__ additions per phase (behavioral tests for each new MCP tool + Go graph fixture + incremental-correctness test)
downstream_consumers: The 11-tool MCP client surface consumed by LLM agents (.mcp.json registration) — additive, must not regress.
coderef-query CLI shares canonical-graph.ts with P1 (no change to CLI; MCP reuses its functions).
ASSISTANT skills that wrap these bins (populate-coderef, coderef-scan, discover) — behavior-compatible; new tools are additive.

risks.title: P2 response bloat — 5,348 unresolved + 1,155 ambiguous source calls could overflow an agent's token budget if listed unpaginated
risks.mitigation: Hard limit + pagination + facet filters (by file, by reason, by relationship) from the first commit; default cap mirrors DEFAULT_LIMIT=25/MAX_LIMIT=100. Responses are for token-budgeted agents — never dump the full set.

risks.title: P5 incremental corruption — the documented trap: filtering elements to changed files blinds buildSymbolTable to unchanged cross-file targets, silently degrading resolved edges to unresolved
risks.mitigation: Persist the FULL project-wide symbol table; on a changed-file delta, re-resolve only changed files AGAINST the full table. Add a correctness test that asserts a single-file edit produces byte-identical resolved-edge set vs a full rebuild (no cross-file resolution loss).

risks.title: P6 Go emitter produces wrong edges — a bad raw-fact walker yields plausible-but-wrong call/import edges (the exact 'system that lies to itself' failure)
risks.mitigation: Follow the resolver's existing discipline: emit unresolved/ambiguous rather than guessing. Add a Go graph-ground-truth fixture (mirroring __tests__/pipeline/graph-ground-truth.test.ts) asserting known Go call/import/export edges resolve correctly and unknowns are explicit unresolved facts — before claiming Go is 'supported'.

risks.title: P4 build-if-missing runs a full populate inside an MCP tool call — could block the agent for minutes on a large stranger repo
risks.mitigation: Emit a clear 'building index, first call may be slow' status; cache aggressively (mtime map already present); consider a size/element ceiling above which it returns a 'run populate first' hint instead of auto-building. Never silently hang.

risks.title: P5 collides with pre-existing watcher WOs (incremental-scan-file-watcher, incremental-scanning-file-watcher, fix-incremental-cache-false-drift)
risks.mitigation: P5-T1 READS those WO dirs + incremental-cache.ts history first to avoid re-solving or regressing prior work. The distinct new contribution here is graph-safe delta RE-RESOLUTION (symbol-table persistence), not file-watching per se.

risks.title: typeOnly traversability (P3) changes edge counts in validation-report — making typeOnly edges resolved/traversable could shift the locked 14-field counts and trip downstream expectations
risks.mitigation: Treat typeOnly as a distinct traversable relationship, NOT by reclassifying it as resolved. Preserve the locked count semantics; add typeOnly as additive. Re-run the full suite + reality anchor after P3.

candidates.note: inbound/resolved-only adjacency map — the single choke point that hides outbound + non-resolved edges from agents (P1, P2)
calleesOf/importsOf/dependenciesOf — outbound functions P1 wires (already built, CLI-exposed, MCP-absent)
shortestPath/allPaths — path functions P1 wires (already built, MCP-absent)
EdgeEvidence union + ambiguous/unresolved edge emit with candidates[]/reason — P2 data source
raw-fact emitters: go/rust/java/cpp default-break at 167-207; TS/JS/PY emitters at 251-688 are the P6 template
incremental honored on scan only; the element-filtering path that must NOT naively feed buildSymbolTable (P5 trap)
full-populate-every-flush; changed-file set collected but only logged, never scoped to the pipeline (P5)

dependencies: src/export/graph-exporter.ts (ExportedGraph type — the compile-time schema guard)
tree-sitter grammars (tree-sitter-go already installed per package.json for P6)
public_api_hits: MCP tool surface (11 tools) — additive expansion to ~15-16 tools; no existing tool signature changes
coderef-watch CLI flags (P5 may add an --incremental-safe flag)

decision_records.title: One WO, all six phases (operator ruling 2026-07-03)
decision_records.rationale: Coherent b+→A+ agent-native narrative; wiring wins sequenced first, heavy builds last, per-phase hard stop between.

decision_records.title: Go is the first language promotion (operator ruling)
decision_records.rationale: Cleanest import/package model and simplest call syntax of the four graph-dead languages — best 'prove the template' choice before rust/java/cpp.

decision_records.title: Engine-rigor bar deferred to a separate track (operator ruling: agent-native first)
decision_records.rationale: Cross-repo resolution + scored graph-correctness eval are a different quality axis; keeping them out preserves this WO's focus and avoids scope sprawl.
